seoTitle: How to Download Your Webflow Site for Free
metaDescription: Extract your Webflow site's front-end code for free using your webflow.io subdomain and a browser-based crawler extension.
intro: Not every project needs a paid plan just to get a copy of the front-end code. This guide covers publishing to your free webflow.io staging domain and using a browser extension to crawl and download the static HTML, CSS, and JavaScript without paying for a code export plan. It's for solo creators who want a portable backup without a monthly subscription.

steps.heading: Publish to your free subdomain
steps.body: Open the publish dropdown in Webflow and select your free webflow.io subdomain to push your latest design changes live.

steps.heading: Open the live staging URL
steps.body: Visit your published webflow.io link in a separate browser tab so the crawler extension has a live page to scan.

steps.heading: Install a free crawler extension
steps.body: Add a browser extension designed for extracting static site files, then launch it from your browser's toolbar.

steps.heading: Scan and download the site
steps.body: Run the extension against your live page to scan the structure, then download the packaged zip of HTML, CSS, and assets.

steps.heading: Clean up the exported files
steps.body: Remove any Webflow tracking scripts or platform badges from the downloaded code, and set up a free external form endpoint for any contact forms.

faq.question: Is this the same as Webflow's official code export?
faq.answer: No, this method relies on crawling your published staging page with a third-party extension rather than Webflow's native export feature, which is limited to paid plans.

faq.question: Will my forms still work after this kind of export?
faq.answer: No, you'll need to swap in an external form endpoint like Formspree or Netlify Forms since native Webflow form handling won't work outside the platform.

faq.question: Is my webflow.io subdomain visible to the public?
faq.answer: Yes, so it's worth noting that anyone with the staging link could technically view your unpublished custom domain content.

faq.question: Are these extensions safe to use?
faq.answer: Popular open-source extensions built for this purpose are generally reliable, but always review permissions before installing any browser extension.
